item.title: BoE's Bailey sees major cybersecurity risks in new Anthropic model
item.description: Central banks and financial regulators must quickly understand the implications of a new artificial intelligence model that could pose major cybersecurity dangers, Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey ​said on Tuesday.

item.title: US set to launch tariff refund system on April 20
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration plans to launch next Monday the system it will use for ​issuing refunds to American importers for $166 billion the companies paid in tariffs that ‌the U.S. Supreme Court struck down in February as unlawful.

item.title: Aurubis CEO expects US copper demand to reduce Comex stockpile
item.description: The massive copper stockpile on the U.S. ‌Comex exchange should be drawn down in the coming months as local demand kicks in, the CEO of German copper producer Aurubis said on Tuesday.

item.title: Theater group says Paramount, Warner Bros merger 'harmful' to industry
item.description: Paramount Skydance's proposed purchase of ​Warner Bros Discovery will consolidate too much power in one company and cause harm ‌to consumers and the industry, the head of a cinema trade group said on Tuesday at the largest convention of movie theater owners.

item.title: American flag maker, others settle US lawsuits alleging bogus 'Made in the USA' claims
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission said it settled three lawsuits accusing ​companies of falsely claiming their products were ‌made in the United States, including a case against a seller of American flags sourced from ​China.

item.title: Amazon's $11.6 billion Globalstar deal will not fix rocket launch bottleneck, analysts say
item.description: Amazon's $11.6 billion deal to buy Globalstar ​bolsters its space ambitions with satellites and wireless airwaves, but will not fix the crippling shortage of ‌rocket launches needed to build out a network capable of rivaling SpaceX, analysts said.

item.title: JPMorgan throws its weight behind Trump administration's push to scrap quarterly reporting
item.description: JPMorgan Chase on Tuesday backed a Trump administration proposal to end mandatory quarterly reporting, with Chief Financial Officer Jeremy Barnum supporting efforts to ease regulatory burdens ​and bolster U.S. capital markets.

item.title: Exclusive: UBS CEO Ermotti may stay late into 2027 due to new rules, succession gap, sources say
item.description: Sergio Ermotti could stay as CEO well ​into the second half of 2027 to steer UBS through a regulatory overhaul and give the board more time ‌to replace him, as an obvious internal successor has yet to emerge, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Macron to host call with EU leaders on social media ban for minors
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron will host a ​video call with other ‌EU leaders and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen ​to push for a ​coordinated approach on banning social ⁠media for minors, Macron's office ​said Tuesday.

item.title: Higher oil prices, higher yields, no more rate cuts? No problem for US stocks
item.description: The U.S. stock market is back to where it was when the Iran war began over six weeks ago, which investors say is a reflection of bets that the conflict will not be long-lasting. But what if that thinking is wrong?

item.title: US Democratic lawmaker pushes for probe into oil trades after Reuters report
item.description: U.S. Democratic Congressman Ritchie Torres on Tuesday wrote to U.S. market regulators ​pushing for an investigation into large oil trades placed just ‌hours before the announcement of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire that Reuters first reported last week.

item.title: Italy court allows class action against Meta over Facebook data scraping
item.description: A Milan court on Tuesday accepted a class action brought ​by a consumer group against Meta ‌Platforms over the theft of personal data suffered by Facebook Italy.

item.title: Anthropic adds Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan to its board
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday added Novartis' Chief Executive Officer Vas Narasimhan to its board of directors, making ​him the first executive from the pharmaceutical industry ‌to join the AI startup's governing body.

item.title: Nestle, Mondelez say were not targeted by EU Commission anti-trust raids
item.description: Food giants Nestle and ​Mondelez said in ‌separate statements on Tuesday they were ​not the target ​of European Commission antitrust ⁠raids carried ​out on Monday as ​part of a probe into a possible ​breach of antitrust ​rules in the chocolate ‌confectionery ⁠market.

item.title: Gunvor's net profits fell sharply in management buyout year
item.description: Global commodity trading house Gunvor recorded an 85% drop in net profits in ​2025 to $104 million, according to financial results ‌released on Tuesday.

item.title: Gucci sales extend falls as Iran war clouds de Meo turnaround
item.description: Sales at Kering's Italian flagship brand Gucci dropped by 8% ‌in the first quarter from the previous year, the fashion group said on Tuesday, as the Iran war curtailed shopping in the Middle East and international travel.

item.title: Banks show trading strength, caution on economic risk
item.description: Wall Street banks reported booming trading revenues after a volatile first quarter but sounded caution about the ripple impact of higher oil and geopolitical risk ​on clients.

item.title: Citadel's Ken Griffin says Iran war threatens global recession
item.description: Billionaire investor Ken Griffin warned on Tuesday that the world could face a ​global recession if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for ‌a prolonged period.

item.title: Disney to eliminate 1,000 positions
item.description: Walt Disney new chief executive Josh D'Amaro announced layoffs in an email to ​employees on Tuesday, as he looks to streamline the company's operations.

item.title: War is over for Wall Street, while oil drags down bonds and gold
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East edges towards its eighth week, financial markets are starting to diverge.

item.title: Boeing jet deliveries slow in March due to 737 MAX wiring issue
item.description: Boeing said on Tuesday it delivered 46 jets in March, down from 51 the prior month, as the company repaired ​damaged wiring in about 25 of its best-selling 737 MAX ‌planes.

item.title: Bessent says confident that core inflation is going down, Fed will need to cut rates
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Tuesday said he ​was quite confident that core ‌inflation would continue to go down in the United States despite the ​Iran war, and repeated his ​call for the Federal Reserve to ⁠cut its interest rates.

item.title: 'Blend' countries pay billions extra as access to cheap multilateral loans narrows
item.description: Developing nations are paying tens of billions of dollars extra to fund ‌infrastructure, education and health projects due to inadequate access to affordable loans from multilateral development banks (MDBs), a report showed on Tuesday.

item.title: Amazon launches AI research tool to speed early-stage drug discovery
item.description: Amazon's cloud unit on Tuesday launched Amazon Bio Discovery, an artificial intelligence application designed to speed early-stage drug discovery by ​allowing scientists to run complex computational workflows without writing code.

item.title: IMF warns Middle East war driving up financial stability risks
item.description: The war in the Middle East is elevating ​global financial stability risks through inflationary pressures that could cause funding markets to tighten, potentially straining non-banks, private credit and ‌artificial intelligence borrowers, the International Monetary Fund warned on Tuesday.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee says rate cuts may need to wait until 2027
item.description: Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee on ​Tuesday said interest-rate cuts ‌may need to wait until 2027, depending on how ​long oil prices ​stay high.

item.title: JPMorgan's private credit exposure was at $50 billion, CFO says
item.description: JPMorgan ​Chase's private ‌credit exposure was ​about $50 ​billion, part of ⁠a ​broader exposure ​of $160 billion in non-bank ​financial ​institutions, Chief Financial ‌Officer ⁠Jeremy Barnum told an ​analysts ​call ⁠on Tuesday.

item.title: IMF cuts growth outlook, warns of potential global recession if Iran war worsens
item.description: The International Monetary Fund cut its growth outlook on Tuesday due to Iran war-driven energy price spikes and supply disruptions and warned that the global economy would teeter on ​the brink of recession if the conflict worsens and oil stays above $100 per barrel through 2027.

item.title: Fed nominee Warsh's holdings include SpaceX, Polymarket, crypto and AI
item.description: Holdings in Elon Musk's SpaceX company and predictions platform Polymarket are among dozens of future-oriented assets that Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh lists on a ​newly filed financial disclosure that shows dozens of apparently small bets on a ‌wide array of emerging and almost science fiction-sounding ventures.

item.title: French group Veolia aims $1.2 bln in revenue from data centres, chips by 2030
item.description: French utility Veolia aims to almost double its revenue ​from providing energy and water ‌management solutions to data centres and chipmakers by 2030, it said on Tuesday, ​betting on strong demand from ​both sectors.
